description: Affrication and shift in place of articulation from a bilabial nasal [m] to a voiced palato-alveolar affricate [dʒ]. This is a rare and complex sound change, likely involving several intermediate steps, such as palatalization followed by affrication.

languageExamples.description: Observed in very rare and specific dialectal contexts where extreme palatalization or consonant mutation rules apply. It is not a widespread or regular sound change.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
